properties.remark: USAF 54-FT WIND TOWER 1007 RECORDED A 37-KNOT WIND GUST OUT OF THE SOUTHWEST AT 300 PM LOCAL.
DAMAGE TO ROOFS, WINDOWS AND CARPORTS OF APPROXIMATELY 30 MOBILE HOMES IN HOBE SOUND. POSSIBLE TORNADO.

A NWS STORM SURVEY CONCLUDED THAT A SEVERE THUNDERSTORM PRODUCED STRAIGHT LINE WIND DAMAGE ACROSS A 1/3 MILE ZONE BETWEEN OLD DIXIE HIGHWAY AND US-1 IN HOBE SOUND. DAMAGE OCCURRED ACROSS 3 TO 4 BLOCKS OF MOBILE HOMES WITH APPROXIMATELY 20 STRUCTURES AFFECTED. MAXIMUM STRAIGHT LINE WINDS ESTIMATED BETWEEN 70 TO 80 MPH. NO INJURIES WERE REPORTED. THE NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE THANKS MARTIN COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT AND THE FLORIDA DIVISION OF EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT FOR THEIR ASSISTANCE.
LARGE TREE DOWN NEAR CORNER OF SYBELIA AVE. AND HILLCREST AVE. REPORT FROM ORLANDO BROADCAST MEDIA. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
SOCIAL MEDIA REPORT OF QUARTER SIZE HAIL IN MOUNT DORA.
TREES UP TO ONE FOOT DIAMETER DOWN. FENCES DOWN IN MIDDLE OF ROAD AND OTHER DEBRIS NEAR INTERSECTION OF E E WILLIAMSON RD AND MARKHAM WOODS RD. IN LONGWOOD
WFTV BROADCAST MEDIA RELAYED AN IMAGE VIA SOCIAL MEDIA OF A LARGE TREE DOWN IN LONGWOOD. TIME ESTIMATED VIA RADAR.
SKYWARN SPOTTER REPORTED QUARTER SIZE HAIL IN KISSIMMEE AT INTERSECTION OF CLAY STREET, PLEASANT HILL ROAD AND HOAGLAND BLVD.
WFTV CH9 RELAYED REPORT FROM SOCIAL MEDIA OF QUARTER SIZE HAIL IN KISSIMMEE AT INDIAN CREEK SUBDIVISION ON POINCIANA BLVD FOUR MILES SOUTH OF 192.

AFTER EXAMINING PHOTOS AND INFORMATION PROVIDED BY EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT OFFICIALS AND WSR-88D RADAR DATA...IT HAS BEEN DETERMINED THAT STRAIGHT LINE WESTERLY WINDS OF 60-75 MPH PRODUCED THE DAMAGE IN SAINT CLOUD WEDNESDAY EVENING. THE DAMAGING WINDS OCCURRED ALONG THE LEADING EDGE OF A RAPIDLY ADVANCING SQUALL LINE. APPROXIMATELY A DOZEN HOMES EXPERIENCED MAINLY MINOR DAMAGE...TO SOFFITS AND ROOF SHINGLES. SEVERAL TREES...FENCES AND POWERLINES WERE ALSO DAMAGED. INFORMATION AND PHOTOS PROVIDED BY THE FOLLOWING AGENCIES ASSISTED WITH THE ASSESSMENT: FLORIDA DIVISION OF EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT...OSCEOLA COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT...AND ST. CLOUD EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT.

SAINT CLOUD EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTED MINOR ROOF DAMAGE TO FOUR HOMES IN SWEETWATER CREEK SUBDIVISION. LOST SHINGLES AND AWNINGS, GUTTERS DOWN, HOLE PUNCHED IN ONE ROOF. EVENT TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
NEW SMYRNA BEACH WEATHER FLOW STATION REPORTED WIND GUST OF 51 KT/59 MPH.
BUOY 41009 REPORTED A 35 KNOT/40 MPH WIND GUST FROM THE SOUTH/180 DEGREES.
ALUMINUM PORCH ROOF OF MOBILE HOME ON TANGELO DRIVE NE PEELED BACK WITH ADDITIONAL ROOF DAMAGE TO DINING ROOM ADDITION.
SPOTTER REPORT AND MANY SOCIAL MEDIA REPORTS OF QUARTER-SIZED HAIL THROUGHOUT SANFORD.

POWER LINES DOWN ON MAGNOLIA DRIVE IN FRUITLAND PARK. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.

ORANGE COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT REPORTS POSSIBLE TORNADO DAMAGE IN THE ISLE OF PINES SUBDIVISION NEAR LAKE MARY JANE. MULTIPLE TREES WERE DOWNED, SOME ON HOMES AND SOME ON ROADWAYS, IN THE SUBDIVISION. ALSO REPORTS OF MULTIPLE POWER LINES DOWN.

NWS SURVEY EXTENDED FROM NARCOOSEE ROAD NEAR EAGLE CREEK GOLF CLUB TO MOSS PARK AND EAST OF LAKE MARY JANE. TO THE WEST OF LAKE MARY JANE STRAIGHT LINE WIND DAMAGE EST.45-55 MPH AT GROUND LEVEL WITH MINOR DAMAGE TO FENCES, SMALL SIGNS AND SMALL TREES. TO THE EAST OF LAKE MARY JANE IN THE ISLE OF PINES SUBDIVISION THERE WAS A NARROW CORRIDOR OF EF-0 TORNADO DAMAGE. WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AT 65-70 MPH AT GROUND LEVEL...BUT WERE ESTIMATED TO BE 80-90 MPH AT TREETOP LEVEL...ABOVE 50 FEET.
SEVERAL PUBLIC REPORTS OF HAIL IN SANFORD UP TO THE SIZE OF HALF DOLLARS. REPORTS VIA SOCIAL MEDIA.
BROADCAST MEDIA RELAYED A PHOTO OF DAMAGE TO A METAL ROOF ON A MOBILE HOME IN THE LAKEWOOD VILLAGE SUBDIVISION IN MELBOURNE. TIME ESTIMATED VIA RADAR.

TRAINED SPOTTERS AND VOLUSIA COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT CONFIRMED A SMALL CESSNA-172 PLANE WAS FLIPPED ON THE DAYTONA BEACH AIRPORT RUNWAY AS A SMALL MICROBURST AFFECTED THE AIRFIELD. REPORTS INDICATE THE PLANE WAS TAXIING ON THE RUNWAY AT THE TIME. WIND GUSTS ESTIMATED OF AT LEAST 45 KNOTS.

STORM SPOTTERS REPORTED THUNDERSTORM WIND DAMAGE ON THE EMBRY RIDDLE AERONAUTICAL UNIVERSITY CAMPUS IN DAYTONA BEACH. MULTIPLE WINDOWS WERE SHATTERED AND BLOWN IN ON THE SECOND FLOOR OF A CAMPUS BUILDING. ALSO, A LARGE TREE BRANCH APPROXIMATELY 5-INCHES IN DIAMETER WAS DOWNED.

INDIAN RIVER COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT RELAYED A REPORT OF TWO WATERSPOUTS APPROXIMATELY 15 MILES OFFSHORE VERO BEACH AROUND 1230 PM EDT SUNDAY APRIL 27 2014. THE WATERSPOUTS FORMED AT THE BASE OF A DEVELOPING CUMULUS CLOUD LINE AND WERE NEARLY STATIONARY. A SPRAY RING WAS VISIBLE AT THE BASE OF ONE OF THE WATERSPOUTS.

A FULLY DEVELOPED WATERSPOUT WAS SEEN TWO MILES EAST OF PATRICK AIR FORCE BASE AT APPROXIMATELY 815 PM EDT. A SPRAY RING WAS EVIDENT AS THE SPOUT MOVED SLOWLY TOWARDS THE NORTH AT AROUND 10 MPH. THE SPOUT DISSIPATED AT APPROXIMATELY 830 PM EDT. MULTIPLE REPORTS FROM STORM SPOTTERS AND SOCIAL MEDIA.

*** 2 FATAL *** ESTIMATED WINDS OF 35 TO 40 KNOTS FOLLOWING THE PASSAGE OF A THUNDERSTORM OUTFLOW BOUNDARY PRODUCED A RAPID STEEPENING OF WAVES ON THE INDIAN RIVER LAGOON. AN 18-FOOT BOAT WITH SIX OCCUPANTS ONBOARD CAPSIZED NEAR THE EAU GALLIE CAUSEWAY AND DRAGON POINT. A 4-YEAR OLD BOY AND AN ELDERLY WOMAN WERE TRAPPED BENEATH THE CAPSIZED BOAT AND DIED AFTER BEING TRANSPORTED TO A NEARBY HOSPITAL.

...DELAYED REPORT...A SEVERE THUNDERSTORM WITH ESTIMATED WIND GUSTS UP TO 60 MPH PRODUCED SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE TO PORTIONS OF THE MERRITT ISLAND HIGH SCHOOL SPORTS COMPLEX. THE BASEBALL FIELD DUGOUT WAS LARGELY BLOWN OVER AND A PORTION THE FOOTBALL LOCKER ROOM AWNING WAS DETACHED FROM THE ROOF. SEVERAL FENCES...INCLUDING THE TENNIS COURT FENCE AS WELL AS THE BASEBALL FIELD CENTER FENCE...WERE ALSO DAMAGED.
AIR FORCE TOWER NUMBER 0300 ALONG HWY 528 CAUSEWAY MEASURED AT THE 54 FT LEVEL.

A WEATHERFLOW STATION JUST NORTH OF SR-520/MERRITT ISLAND CAUSEWAY IN THE BANANA RIVER RECORDED A 46 KNOT/53 MPH WIND GUST OUT OF THE WEST SOUTHWEST. THE STATION RECORDED A SECONDARY WIND GUST OF 35 KNOT/41 MPH OUT OF THE WEST AT 413PM EST.

DONWBURST WINDS TORE A PORCH OFF A MOBILE HOME AND FLIPPED IT ON THE ROOF. A FEW OTHER ADJACENT MOBILE HOMES SUSTAINED MINOR DAMAGE. TIME OF OCCURRENCE IS BASED ON ARCHIVED RADAR DATA AT THE NWS. ORANGE COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT AND WFTV CH 9 CONTRIBUTED TO THIS REPORT.
